lines.english: for those who accept the Guru's Teachings, and become lovingly attuned to the One Lord.
The wandering mind is restrained and held in its place.
The True Name is enshrined in the mind. ||4||
The exciting and intoxicating worldly plays come to an end,
Seeing this, the fire in the water is extinguished.
Serving the True Guru, doubt is dispelled.
They alone realize this, who are blessed by great good fortune. ||5||
Serving the Giver of peace, they become immaculate. ||6||
They know the One Lord, and no other.
Those who are lovingly attuned to the True Lord remain awake and aware night and day.
Selfless service and intuitive awareness come by reflecting upon the Word of the Shabad.
One becomes Jivan-mukta - liberated while yet alive, by listening to the Shabad.
Chanting, intensive meditation and austere self-discipline come by subduing the ego.
I cannot conceive of serving any other.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਨੁ ਮਨੁ ਧਨੁ ਹਰਿ ਆਗੈ ਰਾਖਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: tan man dhan har aagai raakhiaa |
lines.english: I place my body, mind and wealth in offering before Him.
Living a truthful way of life, one finds true peace. ||7||
The Giver of peace is the Eradicator of pain.
Prabhaatee, First Mehl:
Says Nanak, I have tasted the supreme, sublime Essence of the Lord. ||8||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਿਵਲੀ ਕਰਮ ਭੁਅੰਗਮ ਭਾਠੀ ਰੇਚਕ ਪੂਰਕ ਕੁੰਭ ਕਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: nivalee karam bhuangam bhaatthee rechak poorak kunbh karai |
lines.english: You may perform exercises of inner purification, and fire up the furnace of the Kundalini, inhaling and exhaling and holding the breath.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕਿਛੁ ਸੋਝੀ ਨਾਹੀ ਭਰਮੇ ਭੂਲਾ ਬੂਡਿ ਮਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: bin satigur kichh sojhee naahee bharame bhoolaa boodd marai |
lines.english: Without the True Guru, you will not understand; deluded by doubt, you shall drown and die.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਧਾ ਭਰਿਆ ਭਰਿ ਭਰਿ ਧੋਵੈ ਅੰਤਰ ਕੀ ਮਲੁ ਕਦੇ ਨ ਲਹੈ ॥
lines.translit: andhaa bhariaa bhar bhar dhovai antar kee mal kade na lahai |
lines.english: The spiritually blind are filled with filth and pollution; they may wash, but the filth within shall never depart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਮ ਬਿਨਾ ਫੋਕਟ ਸਭਿ ਕਰਮਾ ਜਿਉ ਬਾਜੀਗਰੁ ਭਰਮਿ ਭੁਲੈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naam binaa fokatt sabh karamaa jiau baajeegar bharam bhulai |1|
lines.english: Without the Naam, the Name of the Lord, all their actions are useless, like the magician who deceives through illuions. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਇਆ ਧੰਧਾ ਧਾਵਣੀ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਕਾਰ ਬਿਕਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: maaeaa dhandhaa dhaavanee duramat kaar bikaar |
lines.english: Running around chasing the entanglements of Maya is an evil-minded act of corruption.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂ ਗੁਣ ਸਾਗਰੁ ਅਵਗੁਣ ਮੋਹੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: too gun saagar avagun mohee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: You, O Lord, are the Ocean of virtue; I am so unworthy. ||1||Pause||
The merits of the six religious rituals are obtained through the Immaculate Naam.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਸਾ ਮਾਇਆ ਮੋਹਣੀ ਮਨਮੁਖ ਬੋਲ ਖੁਆਰ ॥
lines.translit: manasaa maaeaa mohanee manamukh bol khuaar |
lines.english: The self-willed manmukh is enticed by his desires for Maya; his words are useless and empty.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੂਰਖੁ ਆਪੁ ਗਣਾਇਦਾ ਬੂਝਿ ਨ ਸਕੈ ਕਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: moorakh aap ganaaeidaa boojh na sakai kaar |
lines.english: The fool makes a show of his self-conceit; he does not know how to behave.

lines.gurmukhi: ਝੂਠੀ ਮਨ ਕੀ ਮਤਿ ਹੈ ਕਰਣੀ ਬਾਦਿ ਬਿਬਾਦੁ ॥
lines.translit: jhootthee man kee mat hai karanee baad bibaad |
lines.english: False is the wisdom of the mind; its actions inspire useless disputes.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਜਨੁ ਝੂਠਾ ਚੰਡਾਲ ਕਾ ਫੋਕਟ ਚਾਰ ਸੀਂਗਾਰ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: majan jhootthaa chanddaal kaa fokatt chaar seengaar |2|
lines.english: The ritual cleansings of the sinner are fradulent; his rituals and decorations are useless and empty. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਨਾਵੈ ਹੋਰੁ ਕਮਾਵਣਾ ਫਿਕਾ ਆਵੈ ਸਾਦੁ ॥
lines.translit: bin naavai hor kamaavanaa fikaa aavai saad |
lines.english: Without the Name, whatever else they do is tasteless and insipid.

lines.gurmukhi: ਝੂਠੇ ਵਿਚਿ ਅਹੰਕਰਣੁ ਹੈ ਖਸਮ ਨ ਪਾਵੈ ਸਾਦੁ ॥
lines.translit: jhootthe vich ahankaran hai khasam na paavai saad |
lines.english: The false are filled with egotism; they do not obtain the sublime taste of their Lord and Master.
Serve the True Guru, and you shall be at peace forever.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਸਟੀ ਸਭਾ ਵਿਗੁਚੀਐ ਬਿਖੁ ਵਾਤੀ ਜੀਵਣ ਬਾਦਿ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: dusattee sabhaa vigucheeai bikh vaatee jeevan baad |3|
lines.english: Associating with their enemies, they are plundered and ruined. Their speech is poison, and their lives are useless. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਏ ਭ੍ਰਮਿ ਭੂਲੇ ਮਰਹੁ ਨ ਕੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: e bhram bhoole marahu na koee |
lines.english: Do not be deluded by doubt; do not invite your own death.
They come and go in reincarnation; they die, only to be reborn and die again. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਹੁ ਸਰੀਰੁ ਹੈ ਤ੍ਰੈ ਗੁਣ ਧਾਤੁ ॥
lines.translit: ehu sareer hai trai gun dhaat |
lines.english: This body wanders, caught in the three dispositions.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਕਿਨੈ ਨ ਪਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: bin satigur mukat kinai na paaee |
lines.english: Without the True Guru, no one is liberated.
Desire and selfishness shall depart from within. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਸੇਵਹੁ ਜਿਸੁ ਮਾਈ ਨ ਬਾਪੁ ॥
lines.translit: so sevahu jis maaee na baap |
lines.english: So serve the One who has no mother or father.
It is afflicted by sorrow and suffering.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਭੇਟੇ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਨ ਹੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: bin satigur bhette mukat na hoee |
lines.english: Without meeting the True Guru, no one is liberated.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਹ ਜਹ ਦੇਖਾ ਤਹ ਤਹ ਸੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: jeh jeh dekhaa teh teh soee |
lines.english: Wherever I look, I see Him.
Enshrine the True One in your heart; this is the most excellent action.
All other hypocritical actions and devotions bring only ruin. ||6||
Inside and out, he knows the One Lord.
When one is rid of duality, then he realizes the Word of the Shabad.
This is the most Excellent Wisdom of the Shabad.
Ashes fall on the heads of those who are in duality. ||7||
To praise the Lord through the Guru's Teachings is the most excellent action.
In the Society of the Saints, contemplate the Glories of God and His spiritual wisdom.
Whoever subdues his mind, knows the state of being dead while yet alive.
O Nanak, by His Grace, the Gracious Lord is realized. ||8||3||
